Each subsequent row has one additional square (▢) starting from the left, replacing an X, while the rightmost column remains entirely X's. Specifically: Row 1 is all X; Row 2 has one ▢ then four X; Row 3 has two ▢ then three X; Row 4 has three ▢ then two X; Row 5 has four ▢ then one X.
